verses.text: Paul, an apostle, to 'Timothy, my dearly beloved son'; grace, mercy, and peace. Tender greeting. He writes near the end with deep love.
Paul, an apostle, to 'Timothy, my dearly beloved son'; grace, mercy, and peace. Tender greeting. He writes near the end with deep love.
I remember thy 'unfeigned faith,' which dwelt first in Lois and Eunice. A godly heritage. Faith passed down through a believing home.
I remember thy 'unfeigned faith,' which dwelt first in Lois and Eunice. A godly heritage. Faith passed down through a believing home.
I remember thy 'unfeigned faith,' which dwelt first in Lois and Eunice. A godly heritage. Faith passed down through a believing home.
Stir up thy gift; 'God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.' Holy boldness. The Spirit gives courage, love, and clear judgment.
Stir up thy gift; 'God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.' Holy boldness. The Spirit gives courage, love, and clear judgment.
Be not ashamed of the testimony; Christ 'hath abolished death, and hath brought life and immortality to light.' Death undone. The gospel reveals life beyond the grave.
Be not ashamed of the testimony; Christ 'hath abolished death, and hath brought life and immortality to light.' Death undone. The gospel reveals life beyond the grave.
Be not ashamed of the testimony; Christ 'hath abolished death, and hath brought life and immortality to light.' Death undone. The gospel reveals life beyond the grave.
'I know whom I have believed, and am persuaded that he is able to keep that which I have committed.' Sure trust. He rests in the One who keeps him.
'I know whom I have believed, and am persuaded that he is able to keep that which I have committed.' Sure trust. He rests in the One who keeps him.
Hold fast 'the form of sound words'; keep the good thing committed 'by the Holy Ghost.' Guard the truth. The Spirit helps us keep what is entrusted.
Hold fast 'the form of sound words'; keep the good thing committed 'by the Holy Ghost.' Guard the truth. The Spirit helps us keep what is entrusted.
Many in Asia turned from me, but Onesiphorus oft refreshed me; the Lord grant him mercy. A faithful friend. He thanks God for one who stood by him.
